## gene_therapies

### GT-mpkp8lda-hi3fa1

- target_genes: VEGF
- target_diseases: Diabetic Macular Edema, Diabetic Retinopathy
- therapy_type: AAV_delivery
- therapy_types: AAV_delivery
- development_stage: approved
- companies: 
- vectors: AAV.7m8, viral-vector, adeno-associated virus serotype 9 (AAV9), AAV, rAAV, viral vectors, adeno-associated viral vectors, AAV vectors, Adeno-Associated Virus (AAV), lentivirus, adenovirus, lipid nanoparticles, gold nanoparticles, DNA nano clews
- key_findings: To evaluate the efficacy and safety of ADVM-022, an AAV.7m8-delivered aflibercept gene therapy, in subjects with diabetic macular edema., Nurses play an essential role in the gene therapy pathway for haemophilia, including education, care coordination, bleed management, and long-term follow-up., Their responsibilities span from eligibility assessment and patient education to peri-infusion care, adverse event detection, and psychosocial support., Gene therapy for haemophilia requires well-trained nurses to ensure safe delivery, effective follow-up, and high-quality patient support., Nurses are crucial for generating real-world evidence through registries and research to evaluate the long-term safety and effectiveness of gene therapy., In vivo CAR gene delivery has demonstrated feasibility and therapeutic potential in recent clinical reports, offering an alternative to the complex ex vivo manufacturing of CAR-T cells., Nurses are critical for generating real-world evidence through registries and research to evaluate the long-term safety and effectiveness of gene therapy., AAV9-Ab immunoglobulin G titers vary by age, with a higher likelihood of titers ≥1:100 in the 0-month age group compared to others., Titers decline up to 12 months of age and then steadily increase from 12 to 24 months., A greater proportion of individuals from Russia had AAV9-Ab titers ≥1:100 compared to other countries., RAAVioli is a robust and versatile computational method for the comprehensive analysis of AAV integration sites and vector rearrangements across long- and short-read sequencing platforms., Short-read sequencing provided superior throughput, uncovering more integration sites and enabling precise quantification of clonal abundance, which is essential for evaluating gene therapy safety., Benchmarking analyses highlighted RAAVioli's higher sensitivity and accuracy relative to existing tools, establishing it as a reliable and scalable resource for AAV-based gene therapy studies., Computational approaches, including machine learning, are enabling the design of finely engineered viral vectors for gene therapy by exploring vast protein sequence spaces beyond traditional methods., These computational methods support viral vector bioengineering by targeting stability, structural modeling, functional prediction, and generating novel variants., The field is moving toward more integrated and holistic design strategies, with a transformative potential for the next generation of viral gene therapies., Lyophilization of AAV vectors with excipients like methyl cellulose (MC) and dileucine (diLeu) maintains their gene expression activity after reconstitution., AAV vectors lyophilized with MC showed similar gene expression patterns in mice as non-lyophilized vectors, with no body weight loss., The combination of diLeu with MC prolonged the gene expression activity of lyophilized AAV vectors for at least 4 weeks at room temperature., Genome editing has evolved from early complex methods like ZFNs and TALENs to the dominant, simpler, and more efficient CRISPR-Cas9 system., Advanced variants like base editors and prime editors enable precise single-nucleotide changes and insertions without double-strand breaks, while emerging tools expand manipulation to RNA and epigenetic modifications., Despite challenges like off-target effects and immunogenicity, CRISPR-derived technologies are at the forefront of next-generation genetic medicine, driving progress toward precise and programmable therapies.

in the oral cavity., Noncoding RNAs (ncRNAs) are promising biomarkers and therapeutic targets for cardiovascular diseases due to their critical roles in molecular mechanisms and consistent dysregulation in these conditions., RNA-based technologies have shown potent efficacy in preclinical studies for targeting cardiovascular disease pathways., Ongoing clinical trials are evaluating of RNA-based gene targeting molecules that can inhibit viral infection., The review summarizes their potential as a therapeutic strategy for oral herpesvirus infections, addressing limitations of developed to more effectively suppress gene expression and show potential as therapeutics for oral infections caused by these viruses.

### GT-4hbmciufjq

- target_genes: OTOF
- target_diseases: hereditary deafness, auditory neuropathy
- therapy_type: gene_replacement
- therapy_types: gene_replacement, AAV_delivery
- development_stage: approved
- companies: Regeneron, Decibel Therapeutics
- vectors: AAV
- key_findings: FDA approved Otarmeni (OTOF gene therapy) in 2025, marking the first gene therapy for genetic hearing loss., Clinical trials showed restoration of hearing in children with OTOF-related auditory neuropathy., This approval represents a major milestone for inner ear gene therapy delivery.

- target_genes: 
- target_diseases: genetic disorders
- therapy_type: CRISPR_editing

- development_stage: preclinical
- companies: MIT McGovern Institute, Broad Institute
- vectors: AAV
- key_findings: NovaIscB: compact RNA-guided enzyme re-engineered from bacteria, 1/3 the size of Cas9, Over 100x more active in human cells than original IscB, with good specificity, Small enough to fit in a single AAV vector — solving delivery challenge of bulkier Cas9 tools, OMEGAoff tool created from NovaIscB demonstrated lasting cholesterol reduction in mice via AAV delivery

- target_genes: TK2

- companies: FDA
- vectors: 
- key_findings: FDA approved Kygevvi (doxecitine and doxribtimine) in 2025 as the first drug for thymidine kinase 2 (TK2) deficiency, a very rare mitochondrial disease, TK2 deficiency causes progressive muscle weakness and respiratory failure; Kygevvi addresses the underlying nucleoside depletion, First-ever approved treatment for this ultra-rare mitochondrial disorder

- therapy_types: gene_therapy, CRISPR_editing, base_editing
- development_stage: regulatory
- companies: FDA
- vectors: 
- key_findings: FDA launched framework (Feb 2026) for accelerating individualized gene therapies for ultra-rare diseases, Draft guidance focuses on therapies targeting specific genetic abnormalities for very small patient populations, FDA will default to one trial instead of two for new drug approvals, Creates pathway for therapies aimed at patient groups too small for traditional clinical trials, Almost 3 dozen gene therapies approved by FDA, including 6 additional CAR T-cell products
- sources: [object Object]
- confidence: {"level":"high","score":60,"source_count":4,"last_validated":"2026-06-04T14:05:08.467Z"}

- last_updated: 2026-06-04T14:05:08.467Z

### GT-0605-encelto

- development_stage: approved

## crispr_applications

### CR-mpkpg9e3-6ad71j

- target_genes: Keap1, PHD2, Nrf2, HIF-1α
- target_diseases: diabetic wounds, chronic diabetic wounds
- editing_type: CRISPR_editing
- development_stage: preclinical
- companies: ASGCT, Innovative Genomics Institute, CRISPR Therapeutics
- key_findings: A CRISPR/Cas9-based gene therapy was developed to simultaneously knockdown Keap1 and PHD2, activating the Nrf2 and HIF-1α pathways to combat oxidative stress in diabetic wounds., Delivery was achieved using multifunctional peptide-modified lysine dendrimers (MsRNPs) that showed excellent biocompatibility and efficient gene editing in vitro., A single topical application of the polyplexes in a diabetic mouse model robustly accelerated wound closure, enhanced collagen deposition, and promoted angiogenesis., Delivery was achieved using multifunctional peptide-modified lysine dendrimers (MsRNPs), which showed excellent biocompatibility and efficient gene editing in vitro., A single CRISPR/Cas9 system was engineered to simultaneously knockdown Keap1 and PHD2, leading to synergistic activation of Nrf2 and HIF-1α pathways., This therapy, delivered by peptide dendrimers, reduced ROS levels and robustly accelerated wound closure, enhanced collagen deposition, and promoted angiogenesis in a diabetic mouse model., A single CRISPR/Cas9 system was engineered for the simultaneous knockdown of Keap1 and PHD2, leading to synergistic activation of Nrf2 and HIF-1α pathways., Topical application of the MsRNP-delivered polyplexes in a diabetic mouse model robustly accelerated wound closure, enhanced collagen deposition, and promoted angiogenesis., This study establishes a novel combinatorial gene-editing strategy and a versatile nanoplatform for treating oxidative stress-related pathologies., The EGOLD method was developed for high-throughput detection of CRISPR off-target effects and identification of chromatin context bias., Off-target effects of CRISPR/Cas9 and base editors strongly correlate with open chromatin regions., Incorporating chromatin context data into machine learning models significantly improves off-target prediction accuracy., Genome editing has evolved from early complex methods like ZFNs and TALENs to the dominant, simpler, and more efficient CRISPR-Cas9 system., Advanced variants like base editors and prime editors enable precise single-nucleotide changes and insertions without double-strand breaks, while emerging tools expand manipulation to RNA and epigenetic modifications., Despite challenges like off-target effects and immunogenicity, CRISPR-derived technologies are at the forefront of next-generation genetic medicine, driving progress toward precise and programmable therapies.
- sources: [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object]
- confidence: {"level":"low","score":15,"source_count":1,"last_validated":"2026-05-25T04:42:37.070Z"}
- first_seen: 2026-05-25T04:27:21.099Z
- last_updated: 2026-05-25T06:26:07.834Z
- vectors: peptide-modified lysine dendrimers (MsRNPs), Adeno-Associated Virus (AAV), lentivirus, adenovirus, lipid nanoparticles, gold nanoparticles, DNA nano clews

### CR-mpkpg9e4-mmdrqv

- target_genes: 
- target_diseases: cancer
- editing_type: CRISPR_editing
- development_stage: preclinical
- companies: 
- key_findings: Lentiviral vectors are being engineered for precise targeting in cancer therapy to overcome limitations of broad tropism and serum sensitivity. Advances include pseudotyping with natural viral envelopes and next-generation retargeting strategies to enhance specificity for immune and tumor cells. Engineered virus-like particles (VLPs) are emerging as precision tools for in vivo delivery of gene editing complexes like CRISPR-Cas9, base-editing, and prime-editing., Lentiviral vectors can be engineered with various pseudotypes and targeting strategies to achieve precise delivery to specific cell types like immune and tumor cells., Next-generation retargeting innovations, including engineered receptor-binding domains and VSV-G mutants, enhance specificity for T cells, B cells, hematopoietic stem cells, and tumor-associated antigens., Engineered virus-like particles (VLPs) are emerging as precision tools for in vivo delivery of CRISPR-Cas9, base-editing, and prime-editing complexes for precision oncology., Lentiviral vectors (LVs) are being engineered for precise targeting in cancer therapy to overcome limitations of broad tropism and serum sensitivity., Advanced pseudotyping strategies using natural and engineered envelopes, along with retargeting innovations, enhance specificity for immune and tumor cells., Engineered virus-like particles (VLPs) are emerging as precision tools for in vivo delivery of CRISPR-based editing complexes for targeted tumor modification and cell-restricted gene editing., Organoids provide physiologically relevant 3D models for biomedical research, addressing limitations of 2D cultures and reducing species-specific discrepancies., Organoids are valuable for propagating hard-to-culture pathogens like human rhinovirus C and norovirus, and for studying viral pathogenesis of SARS-CoV-2 and Zika virus., Organoids have translational utility for antiviral drug screening and preclinical assessment, and can be integrated with technologies like CRISPR gene editing and 3D bioprinting., AI-driven computational models significantly enhance prediction accuracy, algorithmic efficiency, and translational potential in CRISPR-Cas systems for genetic diseases., Deep learning, machine learning, and reinforcement learning approaches are key to these improvements., Ethical challenges, algorithmic bias, and data security remain critical barriers to clinical adoption.
- sources: [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object], [object Object]
- confidence: {"level":"low","score":15,"source_count":1,"last_validated":"2026-05-25T04:42:37.070Z"}
- first_seen: 2026-05-25T04:27:21.100Z
- last_updated: 2026-05-25T04:54:17.046Z
- vectors: lentiviral vectors, VSV-G-pseudotyped LVs, engineered virus-like particles (VLPs)

### CR-mpkpmyef-os0rvq

- target_genes: 
- target_diseases: genetic disorders, oncology
- editing_type: CRISPR_editing
- development_stage: preclinical
- companies: 
- key_findings: Adenoviral vectors are a highly researched gene therapy platform with high transduction efficiency, large transgene capacity, and broad tropism, but their clinical use is limited by immunogenicity, hepatotoxicity, scalability issues, and lack of durable expression., Recent vector engineering innovations, such as rare serotypes, capsid modifications, and third-generation vectors, aim to overcome these challenges., AdVs are being integrated with other technologies like CRISPR-Cas9, nanotechnology, and AI to enhance their precision and adaptability in treating genetic disorders and cancer.
- sources: [object Object]
- confidence: {"level":"low","score":15,"source_count":1,"last_validated":"2026-05-25T04:32:33.444Z"}

### CR-mpkqew9m-9xqkdm

- target_genes: 
- target_diseases: white spot disease, WSD, white spot syndrome virus, WSSV
- editing_type: CRISPR_editing
- development_stage: preclinical
- companies: 
- key_findings: A single nuclei RNA-seq study was performed on the lymphoid organ of Litopenaeus vannamei to understand the impact of WSSV infection., The study constructed the first penaeid shrimp lymphoid organ cell atlas and identified marker genes for different cell types., Multiple genes of interest were identified as potential targets for CRISPR gene editing to develop WSSV-resistant shrimp stocks.

### CR-mpkqew9m-5s6sfl

- target_genes: CD133
- target_diseases: Glioblastoma, GBM
- editing_type: CRISPR_editing
- development_stage: preclinical
- companies: 
- key_findings: Autologous CAR-T cells derived from glioblastoma patients show functional deficits and reduced efficacy compared to healthy donor cells in a pre-clinical model., Allogeneic CAR-T cells, generated using CRISPR to knock out the TCR, demonstrate comparable pre-clinical efficacy to autologous models., This work suggests that allogeneic approaches may be a more effective alternative to autologous CAR-T therapy for glioblastoma.

- editing_type: CRISPR_editing

### CR-tgida5ygo1

- target_genes: PCSK9
- target_diseases: familial hypercholesterolemia, cardiovascular disease
- editing_type: base_editing
- development_stage: clinical
- companies: Verve Therapeutics, Beam Therapeutics
- key_findings: Verve VERVE-101 uses base editing to inactivate PCSK9 in the liver, showing >80% PCSK9 reduction in Phase Ib trial., Beam Therapeutics BEAM-101 for sickle cell disease uses base editing to reactivate fetal hemoglobin., Base editing avoids double-strand breaks, reducing risk of chromosomal rearrangements compared to standard CRISPR-Cas9.

### CR-yolt101-base-edit

- target_genes: PCSK9
- target_diseases: heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia, HeFH
- editing_type: base_editing
- development_stage: clinical
- companies: YolTech Therapeutics
- key_findings: YOLT-101: first in vivo base editing therapy for HeFH in clinical trial, GalNAc-modified LNP delivery targeting hepatocytes, Phase 1 results: 74.4% PCSK9 reduction and 52.3% LDL-C reduction at 24 weeks (0.6 mg/kg cohort), No grade ≥3 adverse events, Published in Nature Medicine March 2026, Dual-targeting mechanism via ApoE-LDLR and GalNAc-ASGPR pathways

### CR-pm359-prime-edit

- target_genes: NCF1
- target_diseases: p47phox-deficient chronic granulomatous disease, p47-CGD
- editing_type: prime_editing
- development_stage: clinical
- companies: Prime Medicine
- key_findings: PM359: first-ever clinical use of prime editing in humans, Corrects delGT in NCF1 gene using prime editing (no double-strand breaks), Two patients treated: 68% and 91% corrected alleles in colony-forming cells, Near-normal neutrophil NADPH oxidase activity restored within 1 month, Stable for at least 6 months, no serious adverse events, Published in New England Journal of Medicine, Prime Medicine winding down CGD program due to small market size

### CR-0601-base-editing-advances

- target_genes: 
- target_diseases: cardiovascular disease, metabolic disorders
- editing_type: base_editing
- development_stage: clinical
- companies: Beam Therapeutics, Verve Therapeutics, YolTech Therapeutics
- key_findings: Significant advancements in CRISPR base editing have boosted editor performance and broadened targeting range, Adenine base editors (ABEs) and cytosine base editors (CBEs) now achieve higher efficiency with reduced off-target effects, Multiple base editing therapies in clinical trials for cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, YOLT-101 (YolTech) achieved 74.4% PCSK9 reduction in HeFH patients, published Nature Medicine 2026, BEAM-302 corrects SERPINA1 Z allele in hepatocytes for AATD treatment

- target_genes: 
- target_diseases: 
- editing_type: CRISPR_editing

- category: crispr_applications
- application_type: AI-enhanced CRISPR design
- description: AI model that accelerates CRISPR guide RNA design and predicts editing outcomes for faster gene therapy development

- development_stage: research

### In Vivo CRISPR Editing 2026 Wave

- name: In Vivo CRISPR Editing 2026 Wave
- category: crispr_applications
- application_type: in vivo gene editing
- description: Major shift from ex vivo to in vivo CRISPR editing with ~250 clinical trials tracked by 2026, using LNP and AAV delivery
- key_features: Approximately 250 CRISPR clinical trials by 2026, Shift toward in vivo delivery approaches, LNP and AAV delivery platforms, Expanding beyond hematological to solid organ diseases
- development_stage: clinical
- sources: web_search
- confidence: high
- first_seen: 2026-06-07T22:08:08.574Z
- last_updated: 2026-06-07T22:08:08.574Z
